reference_impl: official Boltz-2 (torch + pytorch-lightning, GPU -- vast.ai RTX 3090)
reference_commit: boltz 2.2.1 (pip-installed on the vast.ai instance; upstream jwohlzend/boltz)

settings.dtype: bf16-mixed (pytorch-lightning AMP, same precision recipe as the CPU reference; --no_kernels forces the torch-einsum triangle path = the SAME kernel the qb1/qb2 CPU reference uses, only the execution device differs)
settings.target: FKBP12 (FKBP1A, 107 res) + SB3 inhibitor; msa: empty (single-sequence). affinity_pred_value is MW-corrected log10(IC50) in uM, ensemble mean over 5 affinity diffusion samples and the two affinity heads.
settings.execution_device: NVIDIA RTX 3090 24GB (vast.ai on-demand instance, ssh9.vast.ai:16304, label pharma-gpu-ref-migration-rtx3090)

provenance: Generated 2026-07-21 on a rented vast.ai RTX 3090 (on-demand) with the pinned official boltz 2.2.1, --no_kernels so the torch-einsum triangle kernel matches the committed CPU reference (only the execution device differs: GPU vs CPU), same seeds/settings/recycle/sampling/affinity-sample counts as the CPU fixture (nomsa_200step_5affsample_3recycle_bf16_mwcorr). Per-seed wall ~2 min on the 3090. This is the GPU-reference companion to the committed CPU reference, produced so the GPU-vs-CPU reference self-floor (R) can be compared directly (task tt-bio-pharma-benchmark-gpu-reference-migration, Moritz directive 2026-07-21: prefer the GPU reference as the primary comparison point because that is what pharma companies run in production). GPU self-floor R is recorded in docs/implementation-parity-data/boltz2-affinity-{fkbp12,dhfr,tryp}-gpu-ref-floor.json.
invalidation_rule: Regenerate this fixture ONLY when the pinned reference_commit/version changes or the settings above change. For any other change (device seeds, device code, release tag) the fixture is reused as-is and only the device side re-runs.
